AN EF-1 TORNADO DAMAGED AND UPROOTED TREES, SNAPPED A POWER POLE, AND CAUSED ROOF DAMAGE. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED WAS ESTIMATED AROUND 100 MPH. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND LESS THAN 1 MINUTE AND TRAVELED A LITTLE MORE THAN A QUARTER MILE.

STRONGER EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTHWEST OF WINFIELD IA. DAMAGE TO TREES AND CORN WERE NOTED ALONG ITS LITTLE OVER A QUARTER OF MILE PATH. THE MAX WIDTH WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 20 YARDS. THE STRONGEST WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 85 MPH.

A BRIEF HIGH END EF-1 TORNADO SNAPPED AND UPROOTED HARDWOOD TREES AT A FARMSTEAD 1 MILE SOUTH OF ALBURNETT. THE TORNADO WAS 20 YARDS WIDE ALONG ITS 0.12 MILE PATH WITH A PEAK ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 110 MPH. THE TORNADO WAS EMBEDDED IN A ROUGHLY QUARTER MILE WIDE SWATH OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE WITH ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS OF 80 TO 90 MPH.

A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WITH STRAIGHT LINE WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 100 MPH CAUSED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE IN THE MILLEDGEVILLE, ILLINOIS AREA. DAMAGE BEGAN AROUND 3 MILES SOUTHWEST OF MILLEDGEVILLE, AND CONTINUED THROUGH THE TOWN. DAMAGE INCLUDED NUMEROUS LARGE TREES UPROOTED AND SNAPPED, SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO LARGE METAL OUTBUILDINGS, NEAR DESTRUCTION OF A BARN WITH CEMENT BLOCK WALLS, AND A LARGE STEEL STORAGE TANK THAT WAS MOVED SEVERAL FEET. DAMAGE SWATH WAS ABOUT 3 MILES LONG.

POWER LINES DOWN AND ARCING ON 1050 EAST ST, 1 MILE NORTH OF HIGHWAY 92

SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN TOWN...TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
WATER OVER ROADS CAUSED BY HEAVY RAIN. ROADS STARTING TO BE CLOSED IN THE AREA DUE TO WATER OVER THE ROADS.
WATER FROM COFFEE CREEK BACKED UP INTO THE POST OFFICE IN NEW VIENNA.
WATER OVER THE ROADS IN THE COUNTY.
TWITTER PHOTOS OF WATER FROM CREEK OUT OF BANKS AND FLOODING THE ROADWAY WITH WATER UP TO 3 FEET DEEP. CARS ARE STRANDED AND WATER APPEARS TO BE SURROUNDING HOUSES NEARBY.
STREET FLOODING WITH WATER PUSHING MANHOLE COVERRS OFF.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN A FIELD AND FLATTENED CORN AND SNAPPED TREE BRANCEHS THAT WERE ABOUT A FOOT IN DIAMETER BEFORE CROSSING A FIELD AND LIFTING IN ANOTHER CORN FIELD. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1 MINUTE AND TRAVELED 0.71 MILES AND HAD A MAX WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. THE PEAK ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 80 MPH.
LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN AND BLOCKING ROAD - 95TH AVENUE. ONE LIMB REPORTED TO BE HEALTHY AND 1 FOOT IN DIAMETER. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
BURLINGTON ST NEAR VAN BUREN ST WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING. A CAR WAS STALLED IN THE FLOOD WATERS. AS OF 610 AM, IT WAS REOPENED.
IA DOT CLOSED HIGHWAY 21 IN WHAT CHEER, IA DUE TO FLOODING OF COAL CREEK. THE WATER WAS A FOOT DEEP. A DETOUR WAS IN PLACE. AS OF 545 AM IT WAS REOPENED, BUT REDUCED TO ONE LANE.
NUMEROUS ROADS ACROSS STEPHENSON COUNTY; INCLUDING PARTS OF ROUTE 20; WERE WASHED OUT OR UNDER WATER. THE FIRST REPORTS OF FLOODING WERE RECEIVED AROUND 130 AM. FLOODING CONTINUES AS OF 645 AM.
DEEP CREEK CAME OUT OF ITS BANKS FOR FIVE AND HALF HOURS.
IA DOT HAS CLOSED INTERSTATE 80 NEAR WEST BRANCH, IA DUE TO FLOODING. A DETOUR IS IN OPERATION.
EF0 BRIEF TOUCHDOWN OBSERVED IN FIELD BY BROADCAST METEOROLOGIST. NO DAMAGE OBSERVED. TORNADO LASTED LESS THAN 30 SECONDS.
BRIEF TOUCH DOWN NEAR BLARSTOWN. TIME ESTIMATED.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED WATER OVER THE ROAD AT THE INTERSECTION OF EAST HOWARDSVILLE ROAD AND MAMMOSER ROAD, DUE TO HEAVY RAIN OVERNIGHT.
WATER WAS OVER MILLVILLE ROAD.
BRIEF TOUCH DOWN BARELY MISSING A HOUSE. TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 10 TO 15 SECONDS.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 2 SW BARDOLPH. MEDIA RELAYED A SPOTTER REPORT OF A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN.
MEDIA RELAYED A SPOTTER REPORT OF A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCH DOWN. TIME AND LOCATION OF THE EVENT WAS ESTIMATED USING RADAR.
